description: This figure investigates the anisotropic deformation behavior of a low-density Fe-7.95Mn-0.25C-4.94Al medium manganese steel loaded along rolling direction (RD, red curve) and transverse direction (TD, blue curve). Subfigure (a) presents in-situ high-energy XRD patterns at true strains of 0.14, 0.22, 0.3 and 0.38, with FCC austenite peaks (Y₃₁₁, Y₂₂₂, orange diamond markers) and BCC/BCT ferrite/martensite peaks (α₂₁₁, α₂₂₀, green dot markers) marked. FCC peak intensity decreases with increasing strain for both samples, while BCC peaks of RD are higher than TD at high strains, indicating more strain-induced α'-martensite formation in RD. Subfigure (b) shows martensite fraction quantified via Rietveld refinement as a function of true strain, with an inset of corresponding strain hardening rate curves. RD exhibits faster martensite transformation kinetics, with ~4 vol.% higher martensite fraction than TD at true strain of 0.48, corresponding to its higher strain hardening ability. This figure confirms that anisotropic strain-induced martensite transformation is the main origin of the anisotropic mechanical performance of the studied steel.
